what_it_does: Defines a self-contained HTML preview card showing four bronze and tan color examples with names, hex values, and intended usage notes.
observations: Includes @dsCard metadata for group Brand, name Bronze & tan, subtitle Illustration colour, screen-title colour, and viewport 700x220.
Loads shared _card.css and adds local CSS for card spacing, descriptive text, grid layout, swatches, chips, and labels.
Shows bronze #988058 as the starred color for illustrations.
Shows bronze-soft #C9B58A for borders and dividers and tan #D0C098 for illustration shadows.
Uses #988058 again as a screen-title example chip with light text and display font.
standouts: The lead copy explicitly limits bronze use to line-drawn illustrations rather than UI chrome.
The screen-title example demonstrates color usage as rendered text, not only as a swatch.
The card combines visual tokens with role descriptions in the same preview.
conventions: Uses a preview-card HTML pattern with shared _card.css plus file-local styles.
Uses CSS custom properties for fonts, radii, borders, and foreground colors.
Uses inline background styles for each color chip.
Uses compact design-token labeling with name, hex, and usage description.
concerns: Color values are hard-coded in inline styles and repeated in visible text rather than declared once in this file.
The star marker on bronze is visually present but not explained inside the markup.
notes: This file is a compact brand-color preview for bronze and tan roles, especially illustration and screen-title usage.
questions: Does the star after bronze indicate primary status, special approval, or another metadata meaning?
Is screen-title intended to use the same bronze color broadly or only for the shown example?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it presents bronze and tan colors for illustration colour and screen-title colour.
intention_signals: It seems intended to communicate where bronze and tan colors belong in the design system by pairing swatches with short usage constraints and examples.
novelty: The same bronze value is shown both as an illustration token and as a rendered screen-title example, making role nuance visible within one card.
contribution: Contributes a neutral record of bronze and tan brand color roles, including visual swatches, hex values, usage labels, and a constraint that bronze is mainly for line-drawn illustration rather than UI chrome.
summary_for_embedding: HTML design-system preview card documenting bronze, bronze-soft, tan, and screen-title color examples with hex values and usage notes.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for briefing designers or implementers on how bronze and tan brand colors should appear and where they should be used.
